message.content: Build a Jupyter notebook that summarizes the latest v5d stage-2 ROI-quality model run for the S11 cell-quality pipeline. # Context Working dir: `/root/capsule`. Session dir: `/root/capsule/code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/`. The v5d model was retrained 2026-05-01 on **1648 active labels** across 6 subjects (after a fresh round of labelling). The user wants a single self-contained notebook that reports: 1. **What the features are** — describe the v2/v3_extra/v4/v5/v6_vox feature stack and list the top-25 by gain (binary + 4-class). 2. **How labelling data is distributed** — per-subject × class counts table + bar plot. 3. **Representative labels GIF (z-scroll)** — one cell per class (`good`, `bad`, `bad_ok`, `merged`) chosen from the human-labelled set; show z-scrolling 405 + 488 channels with the segmentation mask outlined. 4. **Model performance** — LOSO binary AUC/AP/Brier/acc + 4-class acc/f1_macro per fold, plus the overall confusion matrix. 5. **Predicted-label distribution** — distribution of argmax(4-class proba) on OOF, compared against true label distribution. 6. **Representative predicted-labels GIF** — one cell per *predicted* class chosen by highest confidence p_class on OOF (across all subjects); same z-scroll format. # Inputs (already exist) - Trainer: `code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/05h_train_stage2_v5d.py` (don't run; just reference for the feature design). - Meta: `/root/capsule/code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/roi_quality_stage2_meta_v5d.json`. - LOSO summaries: `code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/stage2/{binary_loso_metrics,4class_loso_metrics,binary_feature_importance,4class_feature_importance,4class_confusion_overall}_v5d.csv`. - Per-subject OOF: `/root/capsule/code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/{sid}_stage2_{binary_score,4class_proba}_v5d.parquet`. - Label log: `code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/roi_qc_actions.jsonl`. - GUI helpers in `code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/gui.py`: - `_load_v5d_proba(sid)` — merges binary + 4class probas keyed on hcr_id (cols: hcr_id, score, p_bad, p_bad_ok, p_good, p_merged). - `_load_all_features(sid)` — merged v1+v5d feature stack with `_pct_subj` columns. - `_make_roi_from_row(sid, row)` — builds an `_ROI` dataclass with bbox/centroid/features. - `_load_roi_crops(roi)` — returns `_RoiCrops` with `img_405`, `img_488`, `label_mask`, all (Z, Y, X), normalized to [0,1]. Uses HCR zarrs from `/root/capsule/data/HCR__*/image_tile_fusing/fused/` and `/root/capsule/data/HCR__*/cell_body_segmentation/segmentation_mask_orig_res.zarr`. - Tight bbox parquet: `/root/capsule/code/dev_code/cached_hcr_cell_tight_bbox/{sid}_hcr_cell_tight_bbox_v1.parquet` (cols: hcr_id, zmin/zmax/ymin/ymax/xmin/xmax_vox, zc/yc/xc_vox). The label log has rows with `{ts, sid, hcr_id, label, ...}` where `label ∈ {good, bad, bad_ok, merged, unsure, _undone_}` and undo rows reference the undone hcr_id via `undoes` dict. The trainer's `_active_labels` does post-undo dedup — mirror that logic in the notebook. Subjects: `["755252", "767018", "767022", "782149", "788406", "790322"]`. Classes: `["bad", "bad_ok", "good", "merged"]` (in that order; binary positives = `{good, bad_ok}`). # Output Create notebook: `/root/capsule/code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/05i_v5d_summary_report.ipynb` GIFs go to: `code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/previews/v5d_summary/` - Filenames like `label_good__.gif`, `pred_bad_ok__.gif`. - Frame: 2-panel (405 left, 488 right). Each panel = mid-y slice or mid-z slice scrolling z through the cell. Use z-scrolling (cycle through z slices of the crop). Overlay the segmentation mask outline (use `find_contours` from skimage or equivalent) in red on both channels for the active hcr_id. - 8 fps, loop. Use `matplotlib.animation.FuncAnimation` + `PillowWriter`. - Title each frame: `sid={sid} hcr_id={hcr_id} z={z}/{Z} class={class}`. # Notebook structure (markdown + code cells) 1. Markdown title + meta (date 2026-05-01, n_labels=1648, model version v5d). 2. Code: imports + paths + helpers. 3. Markdown: **Features**. 4. Code: load meta JSON, print feature column count, count per family (v2/v3_extra/v4/v5/v6_vox/pct_subj), list top-25 binary + top-25 4-class. 5. Markdown: **Labelling distribution**. 6. Code: load + post-undo-dedup label log; print per-subject × class table; plot stacked bar. 7. Markdown: **Representative labels** — explain selection rule. 8. Code: pick 1 ROI per `{good, bad, bad_ok, merged}` from labelled set with the highest model confidence in the **true** class on OOF; generate GIFs; display them inline (use `IPython.display.Image` or HTML `` block). 9. Markdown: **Performance**. 10. Code: load LOSO CSVs; print per-fold + mean (binary AUC/AP/Brier/acc, 4-class acc/f1_macro/per-class f1); plot confusion matrix as heatmap with row-normalized fractions overlaid. 11. Markdown: **Predicted distribution**. 12. Code: aggregate OOF argmax across all 6 subjects (only labelled rows), compare to true label distribution; print contingency table + grouped bar. 13. Markdown: **Representative predicted classes** — highest-confidence prediction per class. 14. Code: pick highest p_class per predicted class across all 6 subjects (must be a labelled OOF row so we know what it actually is); generate GIFs; display inline. Annotate the title with `pred=(p=…) true=`. # Constraints - The notebook must run end-to-end without errors. Run it via `jupyter nbconvert --to notebook --execute --inplace --ExecutePreprocessor.timeout=900` after writing it. Verify all cells executed and GIFs were generated under `outputs/previews/v5d_summary/`. - Add `sys.path.insert(0, "/root/capsule/code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality")` so `from gui import _load_roi_crops, _make_roi_from_row, _load_v5d_proba, _load_all_features` works. - Loading the crops requires zarr — the helpers already do this. Loading 8 cells (4 label + 4 pred) is acceptable; cache them so reruns are fast. - Use the v5d `_pct_subj` source columns from the renamed list: `volume_vox_opened`, `volume_vox_raw`, `axis3d_lambda1_vox2`, `surface_area_vox_opened` — but you only need to be aware of this if you compute pct_subj yourself (you don't, since OOF probas are pre-computed). - For the GIF, downsample if the crop is huge (cap each panel at ~256 px wide via `matplotlib.image` scaling — or just let imshow handle it; cells are usually small). - Don't introduce a new helper file unless strictly necessary; inline functions in the notebook are fine. - Stick to matplotlib + numpy + pandas + skimage + Pillow (already installed). PillowWriter is the GIF backend. # Deliverable - Notebook file written at the path above. - GIFs under `outputs/previews/v5d_summary/`. - Confirm via `jupyter nbconvert --to notebook --execute --inplace ...` that the whole thing runs. - Brief summary back: which 4 labelled cells you picked + which 4 predicted cells you picked + the file paths of the GIFs. # What to avoid - Don't retrain the model. Don't regenerate OOF parquets. Use what's on disk. - Don't add comments explaining what code does — names and structure should be enough. - Don't write a markdown summary file outside the notebook — the notebook is the deliverable. If you hit a blocker (e.g., zarr loading fails on a subject), fall back to a different ROI rather than skipping the section. Report the substitution at the end.

1	"""
2	S11 / Step 5h — Stage-2 GBT v5d: drop µm + add v6 voxel-companion features.
3	
4	Like v5c (drop all absolute-µm features), but also loads
5	`{sid}_features_v6_vox.parquet` which contains voxel-unit recomputations of
6	the 13 µm-only features that had no voxel companion in v5. Tests whether the
7	v5c performance regression was caused by lost feature signal or by the
8	per-sample µm calibration that v5 happened to use.
9	
10	Reads:
11	    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/<sid>_features_v2.parquet
12	    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/<sid>_features_v3_extra.parquet
13	    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/<sid>_features_v4.parquet
14	    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/<sid>_features_v5.parquet
15	    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/<sid>_features_v6_vox.parquet
16	    code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/roi_qc_actions.jsonl
17	
18	Writes (mirrors 05d but with `_v5d` suffix):
19	    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/<sid>_stage2_binary_score_v5d.parquet
20	    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/<sid>_stage2_4class_proba_v5d.parquet
21	    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/roi_quality_stage2_binary_v5d.txt
22	    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/roi_quality_stage2_4class_v5d.txt
23	    code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality/roi_quality_stage2_meta_v5d.json
24	    code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/stage2/binary_loso_metrics_v5d.csv
25	    code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/stage2/binary_feature_importance_v5d.csv
26	    code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/stage2/4class_loso_metrics_v5d.csv
27	    code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/stage2/4class_feature_importance_v5d.csv
28	    code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality/outputs/stage2/4class_confusion_overall_v5d.csv
29	"""
30	from __future__ import annotations
31	
32	import json
33	from pathlib import Path
34	
35	import lightgbm as lgb
36	import numpy as np
37	import pandas as pd
38	from sklearn.metrics import (
39	    accuracy_score,
40	    average_precision_score,
41	    brier_score_loss,
42	    confusion_matrix,
43	    f1_score,
44	    roc_auc_score,
45	)
46	
47	ROOT = Path("/root/capsule")
48	CACHE = ROOT / "code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality"
49	SESS = ROOT / "code/sessions/v3_S11_roi_quality"
50	OUT = SESS / "outputs/stage2"
51	LABEL_LOG = SESS / "outputs/roi_qc_actions.jsonl"
52	
53	SUBJECTS = ["755252", "767018", "767022", "782149", "788406", "790322"]
54	
55	DROP_FEATURES = {"hcr_id", "y", "label", "human_label", "sid"}
56	
57	DROP_UM_FEATURES = {
58	    "axis3d_extent_um",
59	    "axis3d_lambda1_um2",
60	    "axis3d_lambda2_um2",
61	    "axis3d_lambda3_um2",
62	    "axis3d_peak_sep_um_intens",
63	    "axis3d_raw_extent_um",
64	    "bbox_x_extent_um",
65	    "bbox_y_extent_um",
66	    "bbox_z_extent_um",
67	    "c405_core4um_p50_opened",
68	    "c405_shell4um_p50_opened",
69	    "c405_shell_minus_core4um_p50",
70	    "c405_shell_minus_core4um_p90",
71	    "c405_shell_over_core4um_p50_ratio",
72	    "core4um_voxel_frac_opened",
73	    "equivalent_diameter_um_opened",
74	    "equivalent_diameter_um_raw",
75	    "n_neighbors_30um",
76	    "proj_xy_main_extent_um",
77	    "proj_xy_orth_fwhm_um",
78	    "proj_yz_main_extent_um",
79	    "proj_yz_orth_fwhm_um",
80	    "proj_zx_main_extent_um",
81	    "proj_zx_orth_fwhm_um",
82	    "sa_to_vol_um_inv_opened",
83	    "sa_to_vol_um_inv_raw",
84	    "surface_area_um2_opened",
85	    "surface_area_um2_raw",
86	    "volume_um3_opened",
87	    "volume_um3_raw",
88	    "volume_um3_raw_v4",
89	}
90	DROP_FEATURES = DROP_FEATURES | DROP_UM_FEATURES
91	
92	CLASS_NAMES = ["bad", "bad_ok", "good", "merged"]
93	CLASS_TO_IDX = {c: i for i, c in enumerate(CLASS_NAMES)}
94	
95	BINARY_POS = {"good", "bad_ok"}
96	BINARY_NEG = {"bad", "merged"}
97	
98	PCT_RANK_COLS = [
99	    "volume_vox_opened",
100	    "volume_vox_raw",
101	    "axis3d_lambda1_vox2",
102	    "surface_area_vox_opened",
103	]
104	
105	LGB_BINARY = dict(
106	    objective="binary",
107	    learning_rate=0.05,
108	    num_leaves=31,
109	    min_data_in_leaf=20,
110	    feature_fraction=0.85,
111	    bagging_fraction=0.85,
112	    bagging_freq=5,
113	    lambda_l2=1.0,
114	    is_unbalance=True,
115	    metric=["binary_logloss", "auc"],
116	    verbosity=-1,
117	    seed=20260430,
118	)
119	LGB_MULTI = dict(
120	    objective="multiclass",
121	    num_class=len(CLASS_NAMES),
122	    learning_rate=0.05,
123	    num_leaves=31,
124	    min_data_in_leaf=15,
125	    feature_fraction=0.85,
126	    bagging_fraction=0.85,
127	    bagging_freq=5,
128	    lambda_l2=1.0,
129	    metric=["multi_logloss"],
130	    verbosity=-1,
131	    seed=20260430,
132	)
133	N_ESTIMATORS = 400
134	EARLY_STOP = 30
135	
136	
137	def _load_label_log() -> pd.DataFrame:
138	    if not LABEL_LOG.exists():
139	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"label log missing: {LABEL_LOG}")
140	    return pd.read_json(LABEL_LOG, lines=True)
141	
142	
143	def _active_labels(log: pd.DataFrame, sid: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
144	    sub = log[log["sid"].astype(str) == sid].copy()
145	    if sub.empty:
146	        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["hcr_id", "label"])
147	    tomb_ids: set[int] = set()
148	    if (sub["label"] == "_undone_").any():
149	        for _, r in sub[sub["label"] == "_undone_"].iterrows():
150	            ub = r.get("undoes") or {}
151	            try:
152	                tomb_ids.add(int(ub.get("hcr_id", -1)))
153	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
154	                pass
155	    sub = sub[sub["label"].isin(["good", "bad", "bad_ok", "merged", "unsure"])]
156	    sub = sub[~sub["hcr_id"].astype(int).isin(tomb_ids)]
157	    if "ts" in sub.columns:
158	        sub = sub.sort_values("ts")
159	    sub = sub.drop_duplicates(subset=["hcr_id"], keep="last")
160	    return sub[["hcr_id", "label"]].reset_index(drop=True)
161	
162	
163	def _add_within_subject_pct(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
164	    for c in PCT_RANK_COLS:
165	        if c not in df.columns:
166	            df[f"{c}_pct_subj"] = float("nan")
167	            continue
168	        df[f"{c}_pct_subj"] = df[c].rank(pct=True, method="average")
169	    return df
170	
171	
172	def _load_features(sid: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
173	    f = pd.read_parquet(CACHE / f"{sid}_features_v2.parquet")
174	    g_path = CACHE / f"{sid}_features_v3_extra.parquet"
175	    h_path = CACHE / f"{sid}_features_v4.parquet"
176	    k_path = CACHE / f"{sid}_features_v5.parquet"
177	    v_path = CACHE / f"{sid}_features_v6_vox.parquet"
178	    if not g_path.exists():
179	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"v3-extras missing: {g_path}")
180	    if not h_path.exists():
181	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"v4 missing: {h_path}")
182	    if not k_path.exists():
183	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"v5 missing: {k_path}")
184	    if not v_path.exists():
185	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"v6 voxel-companions missing: {v_path}")
186	    g = pd.read_parquet(g_path)
187	    h = pd.read_parquet(h_path)
188	    k = pd.read_parquet(k_path)
189	    v = pd.read_parquet(v_path)
190	    out = f.merge(g, on="hcr_id", how="left", suffixes=("", "_v3"))
191	    out = out.merge(h, on="hcr_id", how="left", suffixes=("", "_v4"))
192	    out = out.merge(k, on="hcr_id", how="left", suffixes=("", "_v5d"))
193	    out = out.merge(v, on="hcr_id", how="left", suffixes=("", "_v6"))
194	    out = _add_within_subject_pct(out)
195	    out["sid"] = sid
196	    return out
197	
198	
199	def _build_matrix(df: pd.DataFrame, feature_columns: list[str] | None
200	                  ) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, list[str]]:
201	    if feature_columns is None:
202	        cols = [
203	            c for c in df.columns
204	            if c not in DROP_FEATURES
205	            and (pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(df[c]) or pd.api.types.is_bool_dtype(df[c]))
206	        ]
207	    else:
208	        cols = list(feature_columns)
209	    X = df[cols].copy()
210	    for c in X.columns:
211	        if pd.api.types.is_bool_dtype(X[c]):
212	            X[c] = X[c].astype("float32")
213	    return X, cols
214	
215	
216	def _early_stop_split(n: int, frac: float = 0.15, seed: int = 0):
217	    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
218	    idx = np.arange(n)
219	    rng.shuffle(idx)
220	    cut = max(1, int(round(n * (1 - frac))))
221	    return idx[:cut], idx[cut:]
222	
223	
224	def train_binary(X_tr, y_tr) -> lgb.Booster:
225	    tr, va = _early_stop_split(len(X_tr), frac=0.15, seed=42)
226	    train_set = lgb.Dataset(X_tr.iloc[tr], y_tr[tr])
227	    valid_set = lgb.Dataset(X_tr.iloc[va], y_tr[va], reference=train_set)
228	    return lgb.train(
229	        LGB_BINARY, train_set,
230	        num_boost_round=N_ESTIMATORS,
231	        valid_sets=[valid_set],

236	def train_multi(X_tr, y_tr) -> lgb.Booster:
237	    tr, va = _early_stop_split(len(X_tr), frac=0.15, seed=42)
238	    train_set = lgb.Dataset(X_tr.iloc[tr], y_tr[tr])
239	    valid_set = lgb.Dataset(X_tr.iloc[va], y_tr[va], reference=train_set)
240	    return lgb.train(
241	        LGB_MULTI, train_set,
242	        num_boost_round=N_ESTIMATORS,
243	        valid_sets=[valid_set],
244	        callbacks=[lgb.early_stopping(EARLY_STOP), lgb.log_evaluation(0)],
245	    )

    "def _make_gif(sid: str, hcr_id: int, class_label: str, title_prefix: str, out_path: Path) -> Path:\n",

    "    crops = _load_roi_crops(roi)\n",
    "    Z = crops.shape[0]\n",

    "    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(6, 3), dpi=80)\n",
    "    ax405, ax488 = axes\n",
    "    for ax in axes:\n",
    "        ax.set_xticks([])\n",
    "        ax.set_yticks([])\n",

    "    def _get_contour_segs(mask_slice):\n",

    "    ax488.set_title('488', fontsize=8)\n",
    "    contours_405 = []\n",
    "    contours_488 = []\n",
    "    fig_title = fig.suptitle('', fontsize=7)\n",

    "        contours_405.clear()\n",
    "        contours_488.clear()\n",
    "        for segs in _get_contour_segs(crops.label_mask[z]):\n",

input.command:
python3 -c "
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

SUBJECTS = ['755252', '767018', '767022', '782149', '788406', '790322']
CLASS_NAMES = ['bad', 'bad_ok', 'good', 'merged']
CACHE = '/root/capsule/code/dev_code/cached_roi_quality'

parts = []
for sid in SUBJECTS:
    b = pd.read_parquet(f'{CACHE}/{sid}_stage2_binary_score_v5d.parquet')[['hcr_id', 'score']]
    p = pd.read_parquet(f'{CACHE}/{sid}_stage2_4class_proba_v5d.parquet')
    merged = b.merge(p, on='hcr_id')
    merged['sid'] = sid
    parts.append(merged)
oof = pd.concat(parts, ignore_index=True)
labelled = oof[oof['human_label'].notna() & oof['human_label'].isin(CLASS_NAMES)].copy()

print('=== Label picks (highest p_true_class) ===')
for cls in CLASS_NAMES:
    sub = labelled[labelled['human_label'] == cls]
    best = sub.nlargest(1, f'p_{cls}').iloc[0]
    print(f'{cls}: sid={best[\"sid\"]} hcr_id={int(best[\"hcr_id\"])} p_{cls}={best[f\"p_{cls}\"]:.4f}')

print()
print('=== Pred picks (highest p_pred_class across all labelled) ===')
for cls in CLASS_NAMES:
    best = labelled.nlargest(1, f'p_{cls}').iloc[0]
    print(f'pred={cls}: sid={best[\"sid\"]} hcr_id={int(best[\"hcr_id\"])} p_{cls}={best[f\"p_{cls}\"]:.4f} true={best[\"human_label\"]}')
"
